title: Dispatcher/Admin job edit modal
description: Add PATCH /api/jobs/:id endpoint for Admin and Dispatcher roles to edit job fields. Add an Edit button to each job row in the Dispatcher Jobs view that opens a modal pre-filled with current values. On save, optimistically update the list and close the modal.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/jobs/:id accepts partial updates for: title, description, scheduledStart, scheduledEnd, schedulingNote, assignedDriverId. Requires Admin or Dispatcher role. Same Zod validation as POST (schedulingNote required if both times are null after merge with existing values). Returns updated job as { data: job }.
PATCH /api/jobs/:id returns 404 if job not found or soft-deleted. Returns 400 for validation errors. createdById is never updated from request body.
JobsPage renders an Edit button per job row (Dispatcher and Admin only). Clicking opens a modal with a form pre-filled with current job values.

Edit modal form fields: title (text), description (textarea), scheduledStart (datetime-local), scheduledEnd (datetime-local), schedulingNote (text, shown with placeholder 'Time to be agreed with customer'), assignedDriverId (select from active Drivers).
On successful PATCH, modal closes and the jobs list updates the edited row without a full page reload. On error, modal stays open and shows the error message.
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors after implementation.
notes: The assignedDriverId select must fetch active users with role DRIVER from GET /api/users (already exists, Admin/Dispatcher can call it). Modal should be a simple CSS Modules overlay — no UI library. PATCH must not overwrite fields not included in the request body; use Prisma's partial update (only include fields that are present in the validated payload). Do not allow editing status via this endpoint — status transitions stay on their existing endpoints.
